name: Embalse del Ebro y Hayedo de Carrales — circular desde Villabáscones de Bezana
description: This moderate 20.9-mile mountain biking route from Villabáscones de Bezana explores the scenic Embalse del Ebro and the magical Hayedo de Ca
region_intro: Mountain bike trails Valle De Valdebezana traverse a diverse landscape characterized by the Montes del Somo and El Escudo mountains, alongside the expansive Ebro reservoir. The region features a mix of solitary moorlands, green pastures, and dense forests of beech and oak, providing varied terrain for mountain biking. Riders can expect routes that navigate through impressive gorges like Desfiladero de Las Palancas and offer significant elevation changes over short distances. This blend of natural features makes the area suitable for a range of mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails in Valle De Valdebezana The most popular mountain bike trail is Embalse del Ebro y Hayedo de Carrales — circular desde Villabáscones de Bezana, a 20.9 miles (33.7 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 55 minutes to complete, offering views of the Ebro reservoir and passing through the Carrales beech forest. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Las Merindades — circular desde Puentedey por Cogullos y Leva, a moderate 17.1 miles (27.5 km) path. This route showcases the varied terrain of the wider Las Merindades region, including technical singletrack and wide tracks. Local mountain bikers also love the Monte de Hijedo — circular desde Arija, a 20.8 miles (33.5 km) trail leading through the Monte de Hijedo forest, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Mountain biking around Valle De Valdebezana is defined by dramatic mountains, lush forests, and the expansive Ebro reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from technical trails to more manageable paths. The routes in Valle De Valdebezana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 180 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Valle De Valdebezana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Valle De Valdebezana?
A: Valle De Valdebezana offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 30 routes to explore. These range from easier paths to more challenging rides, catering to various skill levels.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for mountain biking in Valle De Valdebezana?
A: The terrain in Valle De Valdebezana is incredibly diverse, shaped by its proximity to the Montes del Somo and El Escudo mountains. You'll encounter a mix of solitary red moorlands, green pastures, and dense forests of beech and oak. Routes often feature significant elevation changes over short distances, including technical singletrack, wide tracks, and paths through impressive gorges like the Desfiladero de Las Palancas. Expect limestone areas with rocky sections and damper areas with lush forests.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Valle De Valdebezana?
A: The trails in Valle De Valdebezana cater to various skill levels. There is 1 easy route, 12 moderate routes, and 19 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both beginners and experienced riders can find suitable challenges.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Valle De Valdebezana are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100353781/embalse-del-ebro-y-hayedo-de-carrales-circular-desde-villabascones-de-bezana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Ebro y Hayedo de Carrales — circular desde Villabáscones de Bezana&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1104405553/las-merindades-circular-desde-puentedey-por-cogullos-y-leva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Merindades — circular desde Puentedey por Cogullos y Leva&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while mountain biking in Valle De Valdebezana?
A: Mountain biking in Valle De Valdebezana offers access to stunning natural features. You can ride past the expansive Ebro reservoir, explore dense beech and oak forests, and encounter impressive geological formations. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3912343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Palancas Gorge&lt;/a&gt; and the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4979424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de Las Pisas waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, especially vibrant after rain. The region is also part of the Parque Natural de las Hoces del Alto Ebro y Rudrón, a protected area for its ecological importance.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks accessible by mountain bike?
A: Beyond its natural beauty, the region is rich in cultural heritage. You might encounter charming mountain villages, significant Romanesque architectural legacies, and medieval towers like the Castillo de los Porras in Virtus. Near Arnedo, you can also explore the unique rupestrian hermitages of La Tía Isidora and El Horno.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Valle De Valdebezana?
A: The mountain bike trails in Valle De Valdebezana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the dramatic mountain scenery, lush forests, and the expansive Ebro reservoir, which together create a varied and engaging experience.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for families in Valle De Valdebezana?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation changes and technical sections, there is 1 easy route available, which might be more suitable for families or those new to mountain biking. The varied landscape also offers more manageable paths alongside the more challenging trails, so it's best to check individual route descriptions for suitability.

Q: When is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Valle De Valdebezana?
A: The region offers beautiful riding conditions in spring and summer, with lush green pastures and welcome shade in the dense forests. Autumn is particularly stunning as the beech and oak forests, such as the Hayedo de Carrales, display vibrant colors. While some trails are accessible year-round, conditions can vary, especially during rainy seasons or thaws when waterfalls are most impressive, but trails might be muddier.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer views of the Ebro reservoir?
A: Yes, the Ebro reservoir is a prominent feature of the region, often described as an 'interior freshwater sea.' The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100353781/embalse-del-ebro-y-hayedo-de-carrales-circular-desde-villabascones-de-bezana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse del Ebro y Hayedo de Carrales — circular desde Villabáscones de Bezana&lt;/a&gt; route specifically offers fantastic views of the reservoir while also passing through the beautiful Carrales beech forest.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to eat near the mountain bike trails?
A: Valle De Valdebezana and the wider Las Merindades region are dotted with charming mountain villages. While specific cafes directly on every trail might be limited, you can often find services, including places to eat and drink, in the villages near trailheads or along popular routes. It's advisable to check local amenities before heading out.

Q: Are there any trails that go through dense forests?
A: Absolutely. Valle De Valdebezana is known for its dense forests of beech and oak trees. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100735116/monte-de-hijedo-circular-desde-arija" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de Hijedo — circular desde Arija&lt;/a&gt; lead directly through the Monte de Hijedo forest, offering shaded paths and a beautiful natural setting. The Hayedo de Carrales is another prime example of a stunning beech forest you can experience on two wheels.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Embalse del Ebro y Hayedo de Carrales mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: This circular route starts and ends in Villabáscones de Bezana. You should be able to find suitable parking within or near the village to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter a mix of forest paths, meadows, and sections near the Ebro Reservoir. While parts of the Hayedo de Carrales are generally gentle, some areas, especially around the reservoir, can be muddy and challenging, particularly after rain. Be prepared for potentially demanding ascents to viewpoints.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse del Ebro y Hayedo de Carrales trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate'. While sections within the Hayedo de Carrales offer low difficulty and gentle terrain, other parts, especially near the Embalse del Ebro, can be challenging with mud and demanding ascents. It's best suited for riders with some mountain biking experience and good fitness, especially if conditions are wet.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the stunning Path Through the Hayedo de Carrales, known for its magical atmosphere. The route also includes the Virtus Trail and offers impressive views of the Embalse del Ebro, often described as an 'inland freshwater sea'. During rainy seasons or snowmelt, you might also see the Las Pisas Waterfall.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Hayedo de Carrales is particularly beautiful during autumn when the beech leaves change color. However, be aware that after rain, sections near the Embalse del Ebro can become very muddy and challenging. Spring and early summer also offer lush scenery. Always check the weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Embalse del Ebro y Hayedo de Carrales mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region. However, as this area is part of the Red Natura 2000 network and a Special Protection Area for Birds, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to mountain bike this route. The area is publicly accessible, allowing you to enjoy its natural beauty freely.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino Olvidado (Villasante-Bimón), Ruta de los Sentidos, and the Merindades Bike Race 2022 route, among others.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see in the Hayedo de Carrales and around the Embalse del Ebro?
faqs.answer: The region is rich in biodiversity, being part of the Red Natura 2000 network. The Hayedo de Carrales is home to various forest animals, and the Embalse del Ebro is a crucial wetland for aquatic birds. Keep an eye out for different bird species, and you might spot deer or other forest creatures, especially during quieter times.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near Villabáscones de Bezana?
faqs.answer: Villabáscones de Bezana is a small village, but the surrounding area offers rural accommodation and local eateries. It's advisable to check in advance for specific opening hours or to make reservations, especially during off-peak seasons.

faqs.question: What makes this trail particularly notable for mountain biking?
faqs.answer: This trail stands out for its incredible natural beauty, combining the tranquil, ancient beech forests of Hayedo de Carrales with the expansive views of the Embalse del Ebro. Its varied landscape and ecological significance within the Red Natura 2000 network offer a diverse and rewarding riding experience.

waypoints.name: Path Through the Hayedo de Carrales
